title: India’s app market is booming — but global platforms are capturing most of the gains
description: Non-gaming apps, led by streaming and AI, are driving growth, even as India's spending per user lags global peers.

title: Tesla just increased its spending plan to $25B — here’s where the money is going
description: Tesla's planned capex for 2026 is three times higher than what the company has historically spent. Its CFO said, as a result, Tesla will have a negative free cash flow the rest of the year.

title: Google updates Workspace to make AI your new office intern
description: Google has introduced a host of new automated functions into Workspace, all of which are driven by Workspace Intelligence, its new AI system.

title: Hands on with X’s new AI-powered custom feeds
description: X's AI-powered custom timelines are replacing Communities, with Grok-curated feeds...and new ad slots.

title: Sam Altman’s Orb Company Promoted a Bruno Mars Partnership That Doesn't Exist
description: “To be clear, we were never approached … nor were we in any discussions regarding a partnership or tour access,” a spokesperson for the artist tells WIRED.

title: Crypto scam lures ships into Strait of Hormuz, falsely promising safe passage
description: Ship attacked by Iran after possibly falling for safe passage crypto scam.

title: Tesla reports Q1 2026 earnings: Still profitable
description: Car sales are up, battery sales and emissions credits are down.

title: Our newsroom AI policy
description: How Ars Technica uses, and doesn't use, generative AI.

description: Kennedy's tesimony sets up another clash over vaccines with next CDC director.

title: How SpaceX preempted a $2B fundraise with a $60B buyout offer
description: Cursor was on track to close a $2 billion funding round this week but chose to halt discussions after SpaceX offered a $10 billion "collaboration fee" and a path to a $60 billion acquisition.

title: 5 AI Models Tried to Scam Me. Some of Them Were Scary Good
description: The cyber capabilities of AI models have experts rattled. AI’s social skills may be just as dangerous.

title: AI Tools Are Helping Mediocre North Korean Hackers Steal Millions
description: One group of hackers used AI for everything from vibe coding their malware to creating fake company websites—and stole as much as $12 million in three months.

title: AI needs a strong data fabric to deliver business value
description: Artificial intelligence is moving quickly in the enterprise, from experimentation to everyday use. title: One town’s scheme to get rid of its geese
description: “Pull over!” I order my brother one sunny February afternoon. Our target is in sight: a gaggle of Canada geese, pecking at grass near the dog park. title: There is no nature anymore
description: When people talk about “nature,” they’re generally talking about things that aren’t made by human beings. Rocks. Reefs. Red wolves. title: Join Our Livestream: Musk v. Altman and the Future of OpenAI
description: On May 8, we’re going live to answer your questions about the Musk v. Altman trial that could determine the fate of OpenAI.

title: The Pope’s Warnings About AI Were AI-Generated, a Detection Tool Claims
description: Pangram Labs’ updated Chrome extension puts warning labels on AI slop as you scroll your social feeds.
